title: Lever B — Cross-context calibration transfer + prospective concept-shift detector (LOCO on CD4+ Perturb-seq atlas)
purpose: Lever A proved WITHIN-ASSAY recalibration. This tests whether conformal calibration TRANSFERS across biological CONTEXT (T-cell activation state) WITHIN the same atlas, and whether a LABEL-LIGHT detector can prospectively decide 'recalibrate before you trust' on a new context WITHOUT full held-out labels.
honesty_contract: prereg frozen (sha256) BEFORE any scoring; created_utc EXCLUDED from hash; success criteria stated a priori; an effect counts only if it SURVIVES the pre-specified confound controls AND the detector fires correctly on a HELD-OUT context it was not tuned on; no fabrication; NULL / limit results reported plainly as such; this tests within-ATLAS cross-CONTEXT transfer, not cross-assay transfer.

data.feature_table: data/interim/day2_feature_table.parquet (COMMITTED leakage-safe table; y + features precomputed)
data.h5ad_source: data/raw/GWCD4i.DE_stats.h5ad (33983 contrasts = 11526 genes x 3 culture_condition; var 10282 genes)
data.universe: 11086 genes measured in ALL 3 contexts {Rest, Stim8hr, Stim48hr} -> 33258 aligned rows
data.gene_alignment: gene sets kept ALIGNED across contexts so the tested axis is CONTEXT transfer, not gene novelty

base_predictor.target: y = log1p(trans_effect_magnitude) [L2 of zscore vector EXCLUDING perturbed gene; leakage-safe, same as Lever A]
base_predictor.features_note: context one-hot DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED from features: the held-out context is unseen at train time, so a context indicator would be constant/absent for it. Predictor sees only admissible covariates; context enters only via the LOCO split.
base_predictor.fit_on: TRAIN gene-fold rows from the 2 SOURCE contexts only

design.per_fold: for held-out target context T: SOURCE = the other 2 contexts. Split the 11086 genes into train_genes / calib_genes (gene-disjoint, seed 20260708, 60/40). Fit predictor on SOURCE train_genes rows. Fit split-conformal quantile q(L) on SOURCE calib_genes residuals |y-pred|, method='higher'. TEST = ALL genes' rows in context T (aligned genes; frozen q applied).

metric_A_transfer.definition: does FROZEN source-context calibration hold on the held-out target context? Report empirical coverage @ nominal {0.80,0.90,0.95} on target-context TEST rows, per context and mean-over-folds.
metric_A_transfer.transfer_verdict_rule: HOLDS iff all-fold mean coverage within +/-0.03 of nominal at all 3 levels; BREAKS iff any fold/level deviates > 0.03. If BREAKS, run per-CONTEXT recalibration.
metric_A_transfer.recalibration: per-context recalibration: within target context T, split genes into recal_calib (40%) / recal_eval (60%), gene-disjoint; recompute q(L) on recal_calib residuals; measure coverage on recal_eval. RECOVERS iff within +/-0.03 of nominal.

metric_B_detector.goal: LABEL-LIGHT prospective detector: predict, BEFORE seeing full target labels, whether frozen calibration will FAIL (coverage break > 3pp) on a deployment sub-population of the target context.
metric_B_detector.deployment_unit: stratum = (held-out context T) x (decile of log_baseMean within T); ~10 strata per context x 3 contexts x 3 levels = decision instances

detector_inputs.label_free: domain-classifier mean p(target|x) over the stratum (HistGBC source=0/target=1 on features X only; out-of-fold; NO labels)
detector_inputs.minimal_probe: probe = random <=50 LABELED genes sampled from the stratum; compute |residual| under frozen predictor

metric_B_detector.detector_score: PRIMARY = probe-estimated under-coverage = (nominal L - probe_coverage_frozen(T,s,L)), where probe_coverage_frozen = fraction of the <=50-gene probe with |resid| <= q_source(L). Probe bootstrapped B=200 draws; report mean. SECONDARY (ablation) = label-free domain-shift score alone.
metric_B_detector.decision_threshold: predict BREAK if probe under-coverage > 0.03 (matched to the 3pp break definition)
metric_B_detector.ground_truth: stratum BREAKS iff FULL-stratum coverage (all genes) deviates from nominal by > 0.03 (labels revealed only for evaluation)
metric_B_detector.evaluation: detector AUROC ranking break vs no-break strata using the continuous probe under-coverage score; HELD-OUT protocol: tune any threshold on 2 contexts, evaluate AUROC + accuracy on the 3rd, ROTATE all 3, report mean held-out-context AUROC/accuracy.
metric_B_detector.ablation_baseline: always-trust-frozen = never recalibrate (predict all strata HOLD); accuracy = fraction of strata that truly hold

confounds.report: per-context n, y effect-size distributions, domain-classifier covariate-shift AUC per fold (label-free shift magnitude)
confounds.win_requires: detector fires correctly on a HELD-OUT context it was not tuned on (mean held-out AUROC reported with CI); detector must BEAT the always-trust baseline; recalibration triggered by the detector must recover coverage to within +/-0.03.

success_criteria_a_priori.context_transfer: classified holds / breaks-recalib-fixes / breaks-unfixed by the Metric-A rule
success_criteria_a_priori.detector_positive: detector_works = 'yes' iff (mean held-out-context AUROC > 0.5 with 95% bootstrap CI lower bound > 0.5) AND detector beats always-trust baseline accuracy AND detector-triggered recalibration recovers coverage to +/-0.03; 'partial' iff AUROC>0.5 point estimate but CI touches 0.5 OR too few break events to power it; 'no' otherwise.
success_criteria_a_priori.degenerate_clause: if cross-context transfer HOLDS globally so that break events are near-zero, the detector's SENSITIVITY is untestable; report detector_works='partial' with the honest statement that the correct action is 'trust frozen' and the detector correctly abstains (specificity), and DO NOT claim a sensitivity win that the data cannot support.

novelty_claim_to_adjudicate: label-light PROSPECTIVE concept-shift detection for perturbation UQ -- deciding 'recalibrate before you trust' on a new biological context from unlabeled features + a <=50-gene probe. Adjudicate yes-defensible / borderline / no against 2024-25 prior art.
